title: Affordable Houses for Rent in Cleveland
metaTitle: Affordable Houses for Rent in Cleveland, OH
metaDescription: Looking for affordable houses for rent in Cleveland? See real rent ranges, the most budget-friendly neighborhoods, and Section 8-friendly homes. Book a showing.
secondaryKeywords: low cost houses for rent cleveland
answerBox: Affordable houses for rent in Cleveland typically run $700–$1,250 a month for 2- and 3-bedroom homes, well below the national median. Rent Finder Cleveland manages 90+ rental homes across the city, all accepting Section 8 vouchers, concentrated on the East and Southeast sides where prices tend to run lowest.

sections.h2: What counts as an affordable house in Cleveland?
body: Cleveland is already one of the more affordable rental markets in the country. Per **Zumper's July 4, 2026 report**, Cleveland's median asking rent is $1,250/mo — about **35.9% below the U.S. national median of $1,950/mo**. Within that market, "affordable" for a house generally means a 2- or 3-bedroom home priced from the high-$600s to roughly $1,250 a month.

Rent Finder Cleveland manages **90+ rental homes** — a mix of single-family houses, duplexes, triplexes, and fourplexes — with Cleveland rents ranging **$700 to $1,800/month, typically around $1,000**. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) to see current homes near the affordable end of that range.

sections.h2: How much does an affordable house cost in Cleveland?
body: Two sources illustrate the range renters see. Zumper's median-asking-rent figures (July 4, 2026) sit lower than RentCafe's average-of-professionally-managed-buildings figures (July 2, 2026), because RentCafe's sample skews toward larger newer complexes. For an individual rental house, expect pricing closer to the Zumper end of the range.

rows: Median asking rent, all listing types

sections.h2: Which Cleveland neighborhoods tend to be most affordable?
body: The lowest house rents tend to cluster in Cleveland's East and Southeast side neighborhoods, where much of Rent Finder Cleveland's inventory sits: **Slavic Village (44105)**, **Collinwood (44110)**, **Glenville (44108)**, **Fairfax and Central (44104)**, **Hough (44103)**, and **Buckeye-Shaker (44120)**. We also manage a smaller number of homes on the West side, including **Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102)** and **Old Brooklyn (44109)**. These neighborhoods are covered by multiple **GCRTA bus routes**, and Buckeye-Shaker sits directly on the **RTA Blue/Green light-rail corridor** along Shaker Boulevard.

Compare full rent ranges neighborhood by neighborhood on our [Cleveland neighborhoods hub](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/), or see the citywide picture on our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page.

sections.h2: Section 8 and other ways to stretch an affordable budget
body: For renters using a Housing Choice Voucher, affordability is measured differently — the voucher covers a portion of rent up to CMHA's payment standard for the bedroom size, with the household paying the remainder based on income. **Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready.** Note that Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law and Cleveland itself has not enacted one, so voucher acceptance still varies by individual landlord outside of a few suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, Linndale) with local protections.

Read more in our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/), or check current CMHA income limits directly at [cmha.net](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php).

sections.h2: What else affects total affordability beyond rent?
body: Rent is the largest line item, but a realistic budget also includes utilities and a security deposit. Cleveland electric service comes from either **Cleveland Public Power** or **The Illuminating Company** depending on the address, natural gas is mostly **Enbridge Gas Ohio**, and water is billed through **Cleveland Water** — worth confirming per address since providers vary by block. Winter heating costs run higher than average given Cleveland's lake-effect snow belt climate (roughly 60–70 inches of snow a season), so budget for a seasonal bump in gas or electric bills from November through March.

For move-in costs, Ohio law also governs how a landlord must handle your security deposit, including interest requirements and the timeline for returning it after move-out — see our [Ohio security deposit guide](/cleveland-rentals/guides/ohio-security-deposit-law/) for specifics.

sections.h2: How to find an affordable house right now
body: Availability at the affordable end of the market changes frequently — we typically have dozens of Section 8-ready homes available or coming soon across Cleveland at any given time. Rather than relying on a static list, tell us your budget and bedroom needs and [book a showing](/p/book-showing) for anything that matches, or head straight to [apply](/apply) once you've toured a home you want.

faq.q: What is considered an affordable rent in Cleveland, Ohio?
faq.a: Cleveland's median asking rent was $1,250/mo as of Zumper's July 2026 report, about 36% below the U.S. national median. For houses specifically, affordable generally means roughly $700 to $1,250 a month for a 2- or 3-bedroom home, depending on neighborhood and condition.

faq.q: Where are the cheapest houses for rent in Cleveland?
faq.a: The most affordable house rents tend to be on Cleveland's East and Southeast sides, in neighborhoods like Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker, where much of our own rental portfolio is located.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage low-cost rental houses?
faq.a: Yes. We manage 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland with rents typically ranging from about $700 to $1,800 a month, and a large share fall well within an affordable, budget-conscious range. Every home accepts Section 8 vouchers.

faq.q: Can I use a Section 8 voucher on an affordable house?
faq.a: Yes, on any home we manage — 100% of our portfolio accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Confirm your specific payment standard with CMHA, since the voucher amount depends on bedroom size and household income.

faq.q: Are affordable houses harder to get approved for?
faq.a: Not inherently — screening (income, ID, rental history) applies at every price point. A lower rent can actually make the standard income-to-rent ratio easier to meet. See our guide on the rental application process for what to prepare.
